description: Treats the generation of lower-bound-producing finite automata (as used by Dancik and Lueker) as a policy search in a Markov Decision Process. A Reinforcement Learning agent explores the space of state machines to maximize the expected match rate.
analogical_connections: Designing the bounding automaton is like training an agent to navigate a labyrinth of random bits, where matches are rewards.
implementation_hypothesis: Use Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to train an agent that outputs transition matrices for an N-state automaton, rewarding the steady-state match probability.
experiment_seed: Implement a discrete state RL environment where actions correspond to defining transitions of a deterministic finite automaton.
